_meta.description: Fixed liturgical texts for the Lamentations — Matins of Great and Holy Saturday, served Friday evening
_meta.translation: St. Sergius of Radonezh Cathedral (based on Lenten Triodion)
_meta.note: The Lamentations (Epitaphios service) center on three stases of verses sung around the burial shroud of Christ. Psalm 118 (119) verses are interspersed with composed troparia. The service concludes with a procession with the Epitaphios.
_meta._audit: v0.3.0: Full Lamentations text updated to match st-sergius.org/services/triod/76.pdf. Psalm verses, troparia, evlogetaria, kontakion/ikos, and small litany exclamations aligned to St. Sergius translation. Structural issues (malformed last-verse troparia) fixed. Soft-hyphen artifacts stripped. Typos corrected.

opening.exclamation: Blessed is our God always, now and ever and unto ages of ages.

sixPsalms._note: Psalms 3, 37, 62, 87, 102, 142. Read from psalter.json at assembly time.
sixPsalms.intro:
Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace, good will among men. (×3)

O Lord, Thou shalt open my lips, and my mouth shall declare Thy praise. (×2)
sixPsalms.midGlory:
Glory to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit, now and ever and unto ages of ages. Amen.

Alleluia, alleluia, alleluia! Glory to Thee, O God! (×3)

Lord, have mercy. (×3)

Glory to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit, now and ever and unto ages of ages. Amen.

verses: God is the Lord, and hath appeared unto us; blessed is He that cometh in the Name of the Lord.
O give thanks unto the Lord, for He is good, for His mercy endureth forever.
All nations compassed me about, but in the Name of the Lord I cut them off.
This is the Lord's doing, and it is marvelous in our eyes.

nobleJoseph.label: Troparion
nobleJoseph.text:
Noble Joseph, taking down Thy most pure body from the Tree,
wrapped it in clean linen with sweet spices,
and he laid it in a new tomb.

glory.label: Troparion
glory.text:
When Thou didst descend unto death, O Life Immortal,
then didst Thou slay Hades with the radiant brilliance of Thy Divinity.
And when Thou didst also raise the dead out of the nethermost depths,
all the Hosts of the heavens cried out:
"O Life-giver, Christ our God, glory be to Thee."

now.label: Theotokion
now.text:
The Angel stood by the tomb, and to the women bearing spices he cried aloud:
"Myrrh is fitting for the dead,
but Christ hath shown Himself a stranger to corruption."

stasis1.refrain: Blessed art Thou, O Lord! Teach me Thy statutes.

verses.psalm:
BLESSED ARE THOSE WHO KEEP HIS
TESTIMONIES, WHO SEEK HIM WITH THEIR
WHOLE HEART. (v. 2)
verses.troparion:
In a tomb they laid Thee,
O Christ the Life.
The angelic hosts were overcome with awe
and gloriﬁed Thy condescension.

verses.psalm:
FOR THOSE WHO WORK WICKEDNESS HAVE
NOT WALKED IN HIS WAYS. (v. 3)
verses.troparion:
O Life, how canst Thou die?
How canst Thou dwell in a tomb?
Yet by Thy death Thou hast destroyed the
reign of death,
and raised all the dead from Hell.

verses.psalm:
THOU HAST COMMANDED THY PRECEPTS
TO BE DILIGENTLY KEPT. (v. 4)
verses.troparion:
We magnify Thee,
O Jesus, our King.
We worship Thy Passion and Thy burial,
for, by them, Thou hast saved us from
death.

verses.psalm:
O THAT MY WAYS MAY BE STEADFAST IN
KEEPING THY STATUTES! (v. 5)
verses.troparion:
Earth’s bounds Thou hast measured,
O Jesus, King of all,
yet today Thou dwellest in a narrow tomb,
raising the dead from their graves.

verses.psalm:
THEN I SHALL NOT BE PUT TO SHAME,
HAVING MY EYES FIXED ON ALL THY
COMMANDMENTS. (v. 6)
verses.troparion:
O Messiah, Jesus,
my King, the Lord of all,
whom art Thou seeking in the depths of
Hell?
Hast Thou come to free the race of mortal
men?

verses.psalm:
I WILL PRAISE THEE WITH AN UPRIGHT
HEART, WHEN I LEARN THY RIGHTEOUS
ORDINANCES. (v. 7)
verses.troparion:
Lo, the sov’reign Ruler
of creation is dead.
Almighty God is laid in a new tomb,
to empty the graves of all their dead.

verses.psalm:
I WILL OBSERVE THY STATUTES; O FORSAKE
ME NOT UTTERLY. (v. 8)
verses.troparion:
In a tomb they laid Thee,
O Christ the Life.
By Thy death Thou hast cast down the
might of death
and become the Font of Life for all the
world.

verses.psalm:
HOW CAN A YOUNG MAN KEEP HIS WAY
PURE? BY GUARDING IT ACCORDING TO
THY WORD. (v. 9)
verses.troparion:
Thou hast been numbered
among transgressors, O Christ;
Thou hast justiﬁed us all, O Lamb of God,
by freeing us from the Devil’s works.

verses.psalm:
WITH MY WHOLE HEART I SEEK THEE; LET
ME NOT WANDER FROM THY
COMMANDMENTS. (v. 10)
verses.troparion:
Thou, O Lord, art fairer
than all the sons of men.
Thou hast ﬁlled creation with Thy beauty;
how canst Thou lie before us dead this
day?

verses.psalm:
I HAVE HIDDEN THY WORD IN MY HEART,
THAT I MIGHT NOT SIN AGAINST THEE.
(v. 11)
verses.troparion:
How could Hell bear Thy coming
and not shatter at once?
Death is blinded by Thy splendor, O Lord.
Its gloom is scattered by Thy dazzling
light.

verses.psalm:
BLESSED ART THOU, O LORD! TEACH ME
THY STATUTES! (v. 12)
verses.troparion:
O Jesus, my Salvation,
my Sweetness, my Light!
How art Thou hidden in a dark tomb?
O patience past our strength to
understand!

verses.psalm:
WITH MY LIPS I DECLARE ALL THE
ORDINANCES OF THY MOUTH. (v. 13)
verses.troparion:
The spiritual powers
and the angelic hosts
stand in silence, overcome with wonder
before the awesome mystery of Thy tomb.

verses.psalm:
IN THE WAY OF THY TESTIMONIES I
DELIGHT, AS MUCH AS IN ALL RICHES.
(v. 14)
verses.troparion:
A new and strange wonder!
He who gave me life and breath
now is lifeless, breathless, carried to the
tomb
and buried by Joseph’s hands.

verses.psalm:
I WILL MEDITATE ON THY PRECEPTS, AND
FIX MY EYES ON THY WAYS. (v. 15)
verses.troparion:
In Thy body, buried,
in Hell with Thy soul,
yet not parted from Thy Father’s side, O
Christ!
O strange and awesome wonder!

verses.psalm:
I WILL DELIGHT IN THY STATUTES; I WILL
NOT FORGET THY WORD. (v. 16)
verses.troparion:
O Jesus, all creation
knew Thee to be its Lord!
Thou art truly King of Heaven and earth,
though Thou art shut within a narrow
tomb.

O Christ, Creator,
Thou wast laid within a tomb.
Hell’s foundations quaked and trembled,
seeing Thee
opening the graves of mortal men.

verses.psalm:
OPEN MY EYES, THAT I MAY BEHOLD
WONDROUS THINGS OUT OF THY LAW.
(v. 18)
verses.troparion:
He who holds the earth
in the hollow of His hand
has been put to death and held fast by the
earth,
to save the dead from Hell’s grasping
hand.

verses.psalm:
I AM ONLY A SOJOURNER ON EARTH; HIDE
NOT THY COMMANDMENTS FROM ME.
(v. 19)
verses.troparion:
O my Life, my Savior,
dwelling with the dead in death,
Thou hast destroyed the iron bars of Hell,
and hast risen from corruption.

verses.psalm:
MY SOUL IS CONSUMED WITH LONGING
FOR THY ORDINANCES AT ALL TIMES. (v. 20)
verses.troparion:
Now is God’s ﬂesh hidden
beneath a veil of earth,
yet it is a lampstand brightly shining,
scattering the darkness of Hell.

verses.psalm:
THOU HAST REBUKED THE PROUD, AND
CURSED ARE THOSE WHO WANDER FROM
THY COMMANDMENTS. (v. 21)
verses.troparion:
Joseph, Nicodemus—
and all the heavenly hosts—
hasten to a narrow tomb to enclose Thee,
whom the heights of Heaven cannot
contain.

verses.psalm:
TAKE AWAY FROM ME THEIR SCORN AND
CONTEMPT, FOR I HAVE KEPT THY
TESTIMONIES. (v. 22)
verses.troparion:
Slain willingly, O Jesus,
and laid beneath the earth,
Thou hast raised me up, O Fountain of
Life,
when I lay dead in bitter sin.

verses.psalm:
EVEN THOUGH PRINCES SIT PLOTTING
AGAINST ME, THY SERVANT WILL MEDITATE
ON THY STATUTES. (v. 23)
verses.troparion:
The universe was altered
at Thy Passion, O Word;
knowing that Thou holdest all in unity,
all created things suffered with Thee.

verses.psalm:
THY TESTIMONIES ARE MY DELIGHT; THEY
ARE MY COUNSELORS. (v. 24)
verses.troparion:
When devouring Hades
engulfed the Rock of Life,
in great pain he burst asunder, and the
dead,
held captive from all ages, were released.

verses.psalm:
MY SOUL CLEAVES TO THE DUST; REVIVE ME
ACCORDING TO THY WORD. (v. 25)
verses.troparion:
Thou, O Christ, wast buried
in a new tomb,
and the nature of mortals was made new
when, as God, Thou didst rise from the
dead.

verses.psalm:
WHEN I TOLD OF MY WAYS, THOU DIDST
ANSWER ME; TEACH ME THY STATUTES!
(v. 26)
verses.troparion:
Wishing to save Adam,
Thou didst come down to earth.
Not ﬁnding him on earth, O Master,
Thou didst descend to Hades seeking Him.

verses.psalm:
MAKE ME UNDERSTAND THE WAY OF THY
PRECEPTS, AND I WILL MEDITATE ON THY
WONDROUS WORKS. (v. 27)
verses.troparion:
All the earth was troubled
and quaked with fear.
The daystar hid its brilliant face, O Word,
when Thy great light was hidden in the
earth.

As a man, O Savior,
Thou didst consent to die.
As God, Thou didst raise the dead from
their tombs
and lift them from the dark abyss of sin.

verses.psalm:
PUT FALSE WAYS FAR FROM ME, AND
GRACIOUSLY TEACH ME THY LAW. (v. 29)
verses.troparion:
Tears of lamentation
the Pure Virgin shed for Thee.
With a mother’s grief, O Jesus, she cried
out:
“How can I lay Thee in the grave, O my
Son?”

verses.psalm:
I HAVE CHOSEN THE WAY OF TRUTH; I
HAVE NOT FORGOTTEN THY ORDINANCES.
(v. 30)
verses.troparion:
In the earth’s dark bosom
the Grain of Wheat is laid.
By its death, it shall bring forth abundant
fruit:
Adam’s sons, freed from the chains of
death.

verses.psalm:
I CLEAVE TO THY TESTIMONIES, O LORD;
LET ME NOT BE PUT TO SHAME. (v. 31)
verses.troparion:
As the sun at evening
sets beneath the earth,
Thou art hidden, covered by the night of
death.
O Savior, arise in brighter dawn!

verses.psalm:
I WILL RUN IN THE WAY OF THY
COMMANDMENTS WHEN THOU HAST
ENLARGED MY HEART. (v. 32)
verses.troparion:
As when the moon covers
the circle of the sun,
now the grave has hidden Thee, O Savior,
Thy mortal ﬂesh eclipsed in death.

verses.psalm:
TEACH ME, O LORD, THE WAY OF THY
STATUTES, AND I WILL KEEP IT TO THE END.
(v. 33)
verses.troparion:
By dying in the body,
O Life-giving Christ,
Thou hast delivered mortal men from
death,
bestowing new life upon us all.

verses.psalm:
GIVE ME UNDERSTANDING THAT I MAY
KEEP THY LAW AND OBSERVE IT WITH MY
WHOLE HEART. (v. 34)
verses.troparion:
Through the serpent’s envy,
Adam died of old,
but Thou didst come, O Savior, our New
Adam,
to bring him back to life, through Thy
death.

verses.psalm:
LEAD ME IN THE PATH OF THY
COMMANDMENTS, FOR I DELIGHT IN IT.
(v. 35)
verses.troparion:
When the ranks of angels
saw Thee laid out dead,
they were ﬁlled with fear and wonder, O
Lord,
and covered their faces with their wings.

verses.psalm:
INCLINE MY HEART TO THY TESTIMONIES,
AND NOT TO GAIN. (v. 36)
verses.troparion:
Ramah’s son, O Savior,
took Thee down from the tree.
He laid Thy lifeless body in his tomb;
but rise up as God and save us all.

verses.psalm:
TURN MY EYES FROM LOOKING AT
VANITIES, AND GIVE ME LIFE IN THY WAYS.
(v. 37)
verses.troparion:
Thou art the gladness
of angels, O Lord,
but today Thou hast become their cause of
grief,
as they see Thee in the ﬂesh, a lifeless
corpse.

verses.psalm:
ESTABLISH THY PROMISE IN THY SERVANT,
THAT I MAY FEAR THEE. (v. 38)
verses.troparion:
On the cross uplifted
to draw living men to Thee,
Thou didst descend beneath the earth as
well,
to raise up all that lay buried there.

verses.psalm:
TURN AWAY THE REPROACH THAT I DREAD;
FOR THY ORDINANCES ARE GOOD. (v. 39)
verses.troparion:
O Savior, as a lion
Thou sleepest in the ﬂesh,
yet as a lion’s cub didst Thou arise,
casting off the old age of the ﬂesh.

verses.psalm:
BEHOLD, I LONG FOR THY PRECEPTS; IN
THY RIGHTEOUSNESS GIVE ME LIFE. (v. 40)
verses.troparion:
Thou hast fashioned
Eve from Adam’s side.
Now a spear has pierced Thy side, O
Savior,
and from it ﬂows a purifying stream.

verses.psalm:
LET THY MERCY COME TO ME, O LORD; THY
SALVATION, ACCORDING TO THY PROMISE.
(v. 41)
verses.troparion:
Secretly in Egypt
the lambs of old were slain,
but Thou wast sacriﬁced beneath the open
sky
for the cleansing of the universe.

verses.psalm:
SO SHALL I GIVE AN ANSWER TO THOSE
WHO TAUNT ME, FOR I TRUST IN THY
WORD. (v. 42)
verses.troparion:
What human tongue can utter
this terrible, strange thing?
Today the Lord of Heaven and earth
suffers and dies for our sake.

verses.psalm:
AND TAKE NOT THE WORD OF TRUTH
UTTERLY OUT OF MY MOUTH, FOR MY
HOPE IS IN THY ORDINANCES. (v. 43)
verses.troparion:
“How can we see the Giver
of Life now dead?”
the angels cried in fear and awe.
“How is God enclosed within a tomb?”

verses.psalm:
I WILL KEEP THY LAW CONTINUALLY,
FOREVER AND EVER. (v. 44)
verses.troparion:
New life for Eve, O Savior,
ﬂows from Thy opened side.
Through her I was banished from the tree
of life;
today, she is redeemed with all her sons.

verses.psalm:
AND I SHALL WALK AT LIBERTY, FOR I HAVE
SOUGHT THY PRECEPTS. (v. 45)
verses.troparion:
When Thou, O Jesus,
wast stretched out upon the wood,
Thou didst draw us mortal men to unity;
and from Thy pierced side, forgiveness
ﬂowed for all.

verses.psalm:
I ALSO SPOKE OF THY TESTIMONIES BEFORE
KINGS AND WAS NOT ASHAMED. (v. 46)
verses.troparion:
The noble Joseph,
with trembling and fear,
lays Thy body out for burial, O Lord,
as he gazes with awe on Thy dread form.

verses.psalm:
FOR I FIND MY DELIGHT IN THY
COMMANDMENTS, WHICH I LOVE
EXCEEDINGLY. (v. 47)
verses.troparion:
Thou didst will, O Savior,
to go beneath the earth.
Thou didst free death’s fallen captives from
their chains,
leading them from earth to Heaven.

verses.psalm:
I LIFT UP MY HANDS TO THY
COMMANDMENTS, WHICH I LOVE. (v. 48a)
verses.troparion:
Death seized Thee, O Jesus,
and was strangled in Thy trap.
Hell’s gates were smashed, the fallen were
set free
and carried from beneath the earth on
high.

verses.psalm:
AND I WILL MEDITATE ON THY STATUTES.
(v. 48b)
verses.troparion:
O Savior, Death’s corruption
could not touch Thy holy ﬂesh.
Thou hast bound the ancient murderer of
man,
and restored all the dead to new life.

verses.psalm:
REMEMBER THY WORD TO THY SERVANT, IN
WHICH THOU HAST MADE ME HOPE. (v. 49)
verses.troparion:
Oh, how great the joy,
how full the gladness,
which Thou hast brought to Hades’
prisoners,
like lightning ﬂashing in its gloomy depths.

verses.psalm:
THIS IS MY COMFORT IN MY AFFLICTION,
THAT THY PROMISE GIVES ME LIFE. (v. 50)
verses.troparion:
Thy Passion I worship,
Thy burial I praise,
Thy power I magnify, O loving Lord,
for from corrupting passions Thou hast set
me free.

verses.psalm:
GODLESS MEN UTTERLY DERIDE ME, BUT
I DO NOT TURN AWAY FROM THY LAW.
(v. 51)
verses.troparion:
A sword was sharpened
against Thee, O Christ,
but the sword of the strong foe was
blunted,
and the sword that guards Eden was
turned back.

verses.psalm:
WHEN I THINK OF THY ORDINANCES FROM
OF OLD, I TAKE COMFORT, O LORD. (v. 52)
verses.troparion:
The Ewe saw her Lamb slaughtered
and cried aloud in grief.
She was pierced with anguish, and the
ﬂock of Christ
assembled to join her lament.

verses.psalm:
HOT INDIGNATION SEIZES ME BECAUSE
OF THE WICKED, WHO FORSAKE THY LAW.
(v. 53)
verses.troparion:
Though Thou art buried
in a grave, O Christ,
though Thou goest down to Hell, O Savior,
Thou hast stripped Hell naked, emptying
its graves.

verses.psalm:
THY STATUTES HAVE BEEN MY SONGS IN
THE HOUSE OF MY EXILE. (v. 54)
verses.troparion:
Willingly, O Savior,
Thou didst descend to Hell.
There Thou didst restore the dead to new
life
and lead them back to the Father’s house.

verses.psalm:
I REMEMBER THY NAME IN THE NIGHT, O
LORD, AND KEEP THY LAW. (v. 55)

THIS BLESSING HAS FALLEN TO ME,
BECAUSE I HAVE KEPT THY PRECEPTS. (v. 56)
verses.troparion:
The offspring of Judah
became a poisoned well.
They rejected Thee, the Water of Life,
who ﬁlled them with water from the rock.

verses.psalm:
THOU ART MY PORTION, O LORD; I
PROMISE TO KEEP THY WORDS. (v. 57)
verses.troparion:
As one accused, the Judge stood
at Pilate’s judgment seat.
He was handed over to an unjust death
and suspended on the wood of the cross.

O bloodguilty people,
faithless Israel!
The murderer Barabbas you set free,
but delivered your Savior to the cross.

verses.psalm:
I THOUGHT OF THY WAYS, AND TURNED
MY FEET TO THY TESTIMONIES. (v. 59)
verses.troparion:
Thou didst fashion Adam
from the dust of the earth;
becoming man by nature for his sake,
Thou hast offered up Thy life on the cross.

verses.psalm:
I HASTEN AND DO NOT DELAY TO KEEP THY
COMMANDMENTS. (v. 60)
verses.troparion:
Thou didst obey Thy Father,
O Word of God most high.
Descending to the dread depths of the pit,
Thou hast raised the race of mortal men.

verses.psalm:
THOUGH THE CORDS OF THE WICKED
ENSNARE ME, I DO NOT FORGET THY LAW.
(v. 61)
verses.troparion:
“My heart’s desire, O Jesus!
Woe to me, my Light!
Woe to me, O Light of the world!”
cried the Virgin in her bitter grief.

verses.psalm:
AT MIDNIGHT I RISE TO PRAISE THEE,
BECAUSE OF THY RIGHTEOUS
ORDINANCES. (v. 62)
verses.troparion:
O bloodthirsty people,
vengeful and envious!
Be convicted by the shroud and napkin,
which bear witness to the rising of Christ.

verses.psalm:
I AM A FRIEND OF ALL WHO FEAR THEE AND
KEEP THY COMMANDMENTS. (v. 63)
verses.troparion:
O evil disciple,
murderer of God!
Tell me what poison entered your heart
to make you betray Christ.

verses.psalm:
THE EARTH, O LORD, IS FULL OF THY
STEADFAST LOVE; TEACH ME THY STATUTES.
(v. 64)
verses.troparion:
O money-loving blindness!
You claimed to love the poor,
yet you sold Immortal Ointment for a
price
and earned wages of death and despair.

verses.psalm:
THOU HAST DEALT WELL WITH THY
SERVANT, O LORD, ACCORDING TO THY
WORD. (v. 65)
verses.troparion:
For the Myrrh of Heaven,
what price have you received?
You gained only madness, O cursed Satan,
in exchange for the Pearl of Great Price.

verses.psalm:
TEACH ME GOOD JUDGMENT AND
KNOWLEDGE, FOR I BELIEVE IN THY
COMMANDMENTS. (v. 66)
verses.troparion:
For her soul’s forgiveness,
Mary poured out myrrh.
Judas claimed to love the poor, but envied
her,
and for silver sold the Light of the world.

verses.psalm:
BEFORE I WAS HUMBLED I WENT ASTRAY,
BUT NOW I KEEP THY WORD. (v. 67)
verses.troparion:
O Word of God Immortal,
my joy and my desire,
how shall I endure Thy three days in the
tomb?
My heart is torn asunder with grief.

verses.psalm:
THOU ART GOOD, O LORD; IN THY
GOODNESS TEACH ME THY STATUTES.
(v. 68)
verses.troparion:
“Who will give me water,
a spring for my tears,
that I may weep for my sweet Jesus?”
cried the Virgin Bride of God.

verses.psalm:
THE LIES OF THE PROUD ARE MULTIPLIED
AGAINST ME, BUT WITH MY WHOLE HEART
I KEEP THY PRECEPTS. (v. 69)
verses.troparion:
“O hills and valleys,
the multitude of men,
and all creation, weep and lament with
me,
the Mother of your God.”

verses.psalm:
THEIR HEART IS GROSS LIKE FAT, BUT I
DELIGHT IN THY LAW. (v. 70)
verses.troparion:
“O Joy and Light eternal,
the gladness of my heart,
when shall I see Thee, O my Savior?”
cried the Virgin in her bitter grief.

verses.psalm:
IT WAS GOOD FOR ME THAT THOU HAST
HUMBLED ME, THAT I MIGHT LEARN THY
STATUTES. (v. 71)
verses.troparion:
Thy side was pierced, O Jesus,
like the desert’s rock of old.
From it, O Savior, Wellspring of Eternal
Life,
a stream of living water ﬂows.

verses.psalm:
THE LAW OF THY MOUTH IS BETTER TO ME
THAN THOUSANDS OF GOLD AND SILVER
PIECES. (v. 72)
verses.troparion:
Blood and water ﬂowing
in a double stream
from Thy pierced side as from a single
source
has quenched our thirst for immortal life.

verses.psalm:
GLORY TO THE FATHER, AND TO THE SON,
AND TO THE HOLY SPIRIT.
verses.troparion:
Of Thine own will, O Savior,
Thou wast laid dead in the tomb,
yet Thou art alive, and, as Thou hast
foretold,
by Thy rising Thou shalt raise up mortal
man.

verses.psalm:
NOW AND EVER, AND UNTO AGES OF AGES.
AMEN.
verses.troparion:
O Word of God, we praise Thee,
the Lord of all the world,
with Thy Father and Thy Holy Spirit,
and we glorify Thy burial.

We bless you, O Virgin
Birth-giver of God,
and with faithful hearts we praise the
burial
endured by your Son and our God.

stasis2.refrain: It is right to magnify Thee, O Life-giving Lord.

verses.psalm:
THY HANDS HAVE MADE AND FASHIONED
ME; GIVE ME UNDERSTANDING THAT I MAY
LEARN THY COMMANDMENTS. (v. 73)
verses.troparion:
It is right to magnify Thee,
O Life-giving Lord.
Thou hast stretched out Thy most pure
arms upon the cross
and broken the Enemy’s power.

verses.psalm:
THOSE WHO FEAR THEE SHALL SEE ME AND
REJOICE, BECAUSE I HAVE HOPED IN THY
WORD. (v. 74)
verses.troparion:
It is right to magnify Thee,
O Creator of All.
We are freed from passion through Thy
Passion
and delivered from corruption.

verses.psalm:
I KNOW, O LORD, THAT THY JUDGMENTS
ARE RIGHT, AND THAT IN FAITHFULNESS
THOU HAST AFFLICTED ME. (v. 75)
verses.troparion:
The earth quaked with fear, O Christ,
the sun hid itself,
seeing Thee, the Light that knows no
evening,
sinking down into the tomb.

verses.psalm:
LET THY STEADFAST LOVE BE READY TO
COMFORT ME, ACCORDING TO THY
PROMISE TO THY SERVANT. (v. 76)
verses.troparion:
Thou hast slept in the tomb, O Christ,
a life-giving sleep,
by which Thou hast wakened all the
human race
from the heavy slumber of sin.

verses.psalm:
LET THY MERCY COME TO ME THAT I MAY
LIVE, FOR THY LAW IS MY DELIGHT. (v. 77)
verses.troparion:
“Alone among women, O my Child,
I gave birth to Thee without pain.
Now I cannot bear the grief I suffer,”
the Most Pure Mother cried.

verses.psalm:
LET THE GODLESS BE PUT TO SHAME
BECAUSE THEY HAVE TRANSGRESSED
AGAINST ME UNJUSTLY; AS FOR ME, I WILL
MEDITATE ON THY STATUTES. (v. 78)
verses.troparion:
The seraphim saw Thee, O Lord,
lying dead on earth below
yet united with Thy Father on high,
and they shuddered and trembled with
fear.

verses.psalm:
LET THOSE WHO FEAR THEE TURN TO ME,
THAT THEY MIGHT KNOW THY
TESTIMONIES. (v. 79)
verses.troparion:
The veil of the temple was torn
at Thy cruciﬁxion,
and the lights of Heaven hid their radiance
when Thou, the Sun, wast hidden in the
earth.

verses.psalm:
MAY MY HEART BE BLAMELESS IN THY
STATUTES, THAT I MAY NOT BE PUT TO
SHAME. (v. 80)
verses.troparion:
By Thy nod alone, long ago,
the earth was set on its course.
Now, Thou hast descended dead beneath
the earth.
Tremble, O Heaven, at this sight!

verses.psalm:
MY SOUL LANGUISHES FOR THY
SALVATION; I HOPE IN THY WORD. (v. 81)
verses.troparion:
Thou, who didst form man with Thy hand,
hast gone down beneath the earth,
and with Thine almighty right hand
Thou hast raised up fallen man.

verses.psalm:
MY EYES FAIL WITH WATCHING FOR THY
PROMISE; I ASK, “WHEN WILT THOU
COMFORT ME?” (v. 82)
verses.troparion:
Come, let us sing our lament
to Christ, who dies for us,
that we may be worthy, with the
myrrhbearers,
to hear His saving greeting: “Rejoice!”

verses.psalm:
FOR I HAVE BECOME LIKE A WINESKIN IN
THE SMOKE, YET I HAVE NOT FORGOTTEN
THY STATUTES. (v. 83)
verses.troparion:
Thou art in very truth, O Word,
the Myrrh of Incorruption.
Therefore, to anoint Thee as the Living
God,
the women brought Thee precious myrrh.

verses.psalm:
HOW LONG MUST THY SERVANT ENDURE?
WHEN WILT THOU JUDGE THOSE WHO
PERSECUTE ME? (v. 84)
verses.troparion:
Thou hast destroyed the palaces of Hell
by Thy burial, O Christ.
Thou hast trampled death down by Thy
death, O Lord,
and redeemed earth’s children from
corruption.

verses.psalm:
GODLESS MEN HAVE DUG PITFALLS FOR
ME, MEN WHO DO NOT CONFORM TO THY
LAW. (v. 85)
verses.troparion:
The Source of the River of Life,
the Wisdom of God,
has descended to the utmost depths of
Hell,
to give life to all held captive there.

verses.psalm:
ALL THY COMMANDMENTS ARE SURE; THEY
PERSECUTE ME WITH FALSEHOOD; HELP
ME! (v. 86)
verses.troparion:
“That I may fashion anew
Adam’s broken nature,
I willingly endure Death’s sting in my
ﬂesh.
O Mother, do not strike your breast in
grief.”

verses.psalm:
THEY HAVE ALMOST MADE AN END OF ME
ON EARTH; BUT I HAVE NOT FORSAKEN
THY PRECEPTS. (v. 87)
verses.troparion:
O Morning Star of Righteousness,
Thou hast set beneath the earth,
and hast raised up all the dead as though
from sleep,
dispersing all the darkness of Hell.

verses.psalm:
IN THY STEADFAST LOVE SPARE MY LIFE,
THAT I MAY KEEP THE COMMANDMENTS OF
THY MOUTH. (v. 88)
verses.troparion:
The Seed that gives life to the world,
two-fold in nature,
sown today with tears in earth’s dark
furrows,
tomorrow will sprout forth life.

verses.psalm:
FOREVER, O LORD, THY WORD IS FIRMLY
FIXED IN HEAVEN. (v. 89)
verses.troparion:
Adam was greatly afraid
when God walked in Paradise.
Now, with joy he sees God stalking Hades’
depths.
There he fell, but here he is raised up.

verses.psalm:
THY TRUTH ENDURES TO ALL
GENERATIONS. THOU HAST ESTABLISHED
THE EARTH, AND IT STANDS FAST. (v. 90)
verses.troparion:
She who gave birth to Thee, O Christ,
poured out her tears for Thee;
when she saw Thy body buried in the
tomb,
“Arise, as Thou hast promised!” she cried.

verses.psalm:
THE DAY CONTINUES BY THY ORDINANCE,
FOR ALL THINGS ARE THY SERVANTS. (v. 91)
verses.troparion:
In a new tomb, O Savior,
Joseph laid Thee reverently,
singing lamentations for Thy Exodus,
mingled with the voices mourning for
Thee.

verses.psalm:
IF THY LAW HAD NOT BEEN MY DELIGHT,
I SHOULD HAVE PERISHED IN MY
AFFLICTION. (v. 92)
verses.troparion:
When Thy Mother saw Thee, O Christ,
pierced with nails upon the cross,
she was smitten with the nails of bitter
grief;
her soul was pierced, as Simeon foretold.

verses.psalm:
I WILL NEVER FORGET THY PRECEPTS; FOR
BY THEM THOU HAST GIVEN ME LIFE. (v. 93)
verses.troparion:
O Sweetness of the universe,
when Thy Mother saw Thee
drink the bitter vinegar upon the cross,
her cheeks were wet with bitter tears.

verses.psalm:
I AM THINE, SAVE ME, FOR I HAVE SOUGHT
THY PRECEPTS. (v. 94)
verses.troparion:
“I am torn asunder with grief;
my heart is wounded unto death
seeing Thee unjustly slaughtered, O my
Son,”
said the All-pure Virgin in her tears.

verses.psalm:
THE WICKED LIE IN WAIT TO DESTROY ME,
BUT I CONSIDER THY TESTIMONIES. (v. 95)
verses.troparion:
“How shall I close Thy lips, O Word,
and Thine eyes, so sweet?
How shall I prepare Thy body for the
tomb?”
Joseph cried in trembling.

verses.psalm:
I HAVE SEEN THAT ALL THINGS COME TO AN
END, BUT THY COMMANDMENT ALONE IS
ETERNAL. (v. 96)
verses.troparion:
Joseph and Nicodemus
now sing hymns of burial
as they stand before the tomb of Christ the
Lord,
and with them sing the seraphim.

verses.psalm:
OH, HOW I LOVE THY LAW, O LORD! IT IS
MY MEDITATION ALL DAY LONG. (v. 97)
verses.troparion:
O Savior, Sun of Righteousness,
Thou hast set beneath the earth;
and the moon, Thy Mother, is eclipsed
with grief,
suffering the loss of Thy light.

verses.psalm:
THY COMMANDMENT MAKES ME WISER
THAN MY ENEMIES, FOR IT IS MINE
FOREVER. (v. 98)
verses.troparion:
Hell shook with fear, O Savior,
seeing Thee, the Giver of Life,
spoiling him of all his hoarded riches
and raising up the dead from every age.

verses.psalm:
I HAVE MORE UNDERSTANDING THAN ALL
MY TEACHERS, FOR THY TESTIMONIES ARE
MY MEDITATION. (v. 99)
verses.troparion:
The sun rises brilliantly, O Word,
when the night is passed.
And Thou, after Thy death, shall rise in
splendor
as a bridegroom coming from his
chambers.

verses.psalm:
I UNDERSTAND MORE THAN MY ELDERS,
FOR I SEEK THY COMMANDMENTS. (v. 100)
verses.troparion:
Earth shook and quaked in fear,
O Savior and Creator,
when in her dark bosom she enfolded
Thee,
and with her quaking she awoke the dead.

verses.psalm:
I HOLD BACK MY FEET FROM EVERY EVIL
WAY, IN ORDER TO KEEP THY WORD. (v.101)
verses.troparion:
Joseph, that righteous man,
with Nicodemus,
wrapped Thee in sweet spices as they cried
aloud:
“Be terriﬁed and tremble, O earth!”

verses.psalm:
I DO NOT TURN ASIDE FROM THY
ORDINANCES, FOR THOU HAST TAUGHT
ME. (v. 102)
verses.troparion:
The sun set together with Thee,
O Creator of the light.
All creation was then overcome with fear
and confessed Thee as Creator.

verses.psalm:
HOW SWEET ARE THY WORDS TO MY TASTE,
SWEETER THAN HONEY TO MY MOUTH!
(v. 103)
verses.troparion:
A stone hewn by human hands
conceals the Cornerstone,
and a mortal man hides God within a
tomb.
Be shaken and tremble, O Earth!

verses.psalm:
THROUGH THY PRECEPTS I GAIN
UNDERSTANDING; THEREFORE I HATE
EVERY FALSE WAY. (v. 104)
verses.troparion:
“Behold the disciple Thou hast loved
and Thine own Mother!
Let us hear Thy voice again, my sweetest
Child,”
cried the Pure One, weeping.

verses.psalm:
THY WORD IS A LAMP TO MY FEET, AND A
LIGHT TO MY PATH. (v. 105)
verses.troparion:
Though Thy people nailed Thee to the
cross,
Thou didst not destroy them
but raised up their fathers from the dead,
O Word,
as Thou art the Giver of Life.

verses.psalm:
I HAVE SWORN AN OATH AND CONFIRMED
IT, TO OBSERVE THY RIGHTEOUS
ORDINANCES. (v. 106)
verses.troparion:
No form or beauty didst Thou have
when Thou didst suffer,
but when Thou wast clothed in
resurrection light,
Thou didst beautify the human race.

verses.psalm:
I AM SORELY AFFLICTED; GIVE ME LIFE, O
LORD, ACCORDING TO THY WORD. (v. 107)
verses.troparion:
Thou hast gone down beneath the earth,
O Daystar without evening,
and the sun was darkened at the height of
noon,
unable to endure the sight of Thee.

verses.psalm:
ACCEPT MY OFFERINGS OF PRAISE, O LORD,
AND TEACH ME THY ORDINANCES. (v. 108)
verses.troparion:
Clothed in black robes of mourning,
like faithful servants,
Sun and Moon together, O my Savior,
were darkened completely at Thy death.

verses.psalm:
MY SOUL IS CONTINUALLY IN THY HANDS,
AND I HAVE NOT FORGOTTEN THY LAW.
(v. 109)
verses.troparion:
The centurion knew Thee to be God,
though Thou wast cold in death.
“How then shall I touch Thee with my
hands, my God?
I am afraid!” Joseph cried.

verses.psalm:
THE WICKED HAVE LAID A SNARE FOR ME,
BUT I DO NOT STRAY FROM THY PRECEPTS.
(v. 110)
verses.troparion:
Adam slept, and from his side came Eve,
who brought death to all ﬂesh.
Thou hast slept a blessed sleep, O Word of
God,
and from Thy side ﬂows life for all the
world.

verses.psalm:
THY TESTIMONIES ARE MY HERITAGE
FOREVER; YEA, THEY ARE THE JOY OF MY
HEART. (v. 111)
verses.troparion:
Thou hast slept a little while
and brought the dead to life.
Thou hast raised up all who slept from
every age
by Thy resurrection, O loving Lord.

verses.psalm:
I INCLINE MY HEART TO PERFORM THY
STATUTES FOREVER, TO THE END. (v. 112)
verses.troparion:
O Life-giving Vine,
Thou wast lifted from the earth.
There Thou didst pour out Thy saving
Wine, O Lord.
I glorify Thy Passion and Thy Cross!

verses.psalm:
I HAVE HATED TRANSGRESSORS, BUT THY
LAW I HAVE LOVED. (v. 113)
verses.troparion:
When the captains of the angels
saw Thee, O Savior,
naked, stained with blood, condemned
unjustly,
how could they bear such murderous
cruelty?

verses.psalm:
THOU ART MY HIDING PLACE AND MY
DEFENDER; I HOPE IN THY WORD. (v. 114)
verses.troparion:
O deceitful people,
unbelieving Israel,
knowing that His temple would be raised
again,
why did you condemn Christ to the cross?

verses.psalm:
DEPART FROM ME, YOU EVILDOERS, AND I
WILL KEEP THE COMMANDMENTS OF MY
GOD. (v. 115)
verses.troparion:
You clothe Him who orders all things
in a robe of mockery,
though He ﬁlled the vault of heaven with
bright stars
and made the earth most wondrously.

verses.psalm:
UPHOLD ME ACCORDING TO THY
PROMISE, THAT I MAY LIVE, AND LET ME
NOT BE PUT TO SHAME IN MY HOPE.
(v. 116)
verses.troparion:
Wounded in Thy side, O Word,
like the pelican
Thou hast given life to Thy dead children,
with the life-giving drops of Thy blood.

verses.psalm:
HELP ME, AND I SHALL BE SAVED, AND I
WILL MEDITATE ON THY STATUTES
CONTINUALLY. (v. 117)
verses.troparion:
Joshua made the sun stand still of old
as he smote the heathen tribes;
Thou, O Lord, hast blotted out its
brightness
when Thou didst crush the Prince of
Darkness.

verses.psalm:
THOU DOST SPURN ALL WHO GO ASTRAY
FROM THY STATUTES; YEA, THEIR CUNNING
IS IN VAIN. (v. 118)
verses.troparion:
Thou hast consented in Thy love
to become a mortal man;
without being parted from Thy Father’s
side,
Thou hast gone down to Hades, O Christ.

verses.psalm:
I HAVE REGARDED ALL THE WICKED OF THE
EARTH AS TRANSGRESSORS; THEREFORE I
LOVE THY TESTIMONIES. (v. 119)
verses.troparion:
He who hung the earth upon the ﬂoods
is hung upon the cross.
As a lifeless corpse, the earth receives Him
and quakes in terror at His presence.

verses.psalm:
NAIL MY FLESH WITH THE FEAR OF THEE,
FOR I AM AFRAID OF THY JUDGMENTS.
(v. 120)
verses.troparion:
“O my beloved Son,”
laments the Virgin,
“I now see Thee hanging on the cross
condemned,
whom I had hoped to see enthroned as
King.”

“Gabriel announced to me this news
when he ﬂew down from heaven;
he said that the reign of my Son Jesus
would endure forever.”

verses.psalm:
UPHOLD THE WELFARE OF THY SERVANT;
LET NOT THE GODLESS OPPRESS ME. (v. 122)
verses.troparion:
“Simeon’s prophecy
has been fulﬁlled today,
for the sword of which he spoke has
pierced my heart,
O my Son, Emmanuel.”

verses.psalm:
MY EYES FAIL WITH WATCHING FOR THY
SALVATION, AND FOR THE FULFILLMENT OF
THY RIGHTEOUS PROMISE. (v. 123)
verses.troparion:
Be ashamed, O Jews,
at the witness of your dead,
raised to life by Him whom you
condemned to death
in malice and envy.

verses.psalm:
DEAL WITH THY SERVANT ACCORDING TO
THY MERCY, AND TEACH ME THY STATUTES.
(v. 124)
verses.troparion:
O my Jesus, Light invisible,
when the Sun beheld Thee
hidden without breath inside a tomb of
stone,
it trembled and darkened its light.

verses.psalm:
I AM THY SERVANT; GIVE ME
UNDERSTANDING, THAT I MAY KNOW THY
TESTIMONIES. (v. 125)
verses.troparion:
Thine All-blameless Mother
wept most bitterly,
when she saw Thee laid within the tomb,
O Word,
ineffable, eternal God.

verses.psalm:
IT IS TIME FOR THE LORD TO ACT, FOR THEY
HAVE BROKEN THY LAW. (v. 126)
verses.troparion:
Thine undeﬁled Mother,
when she saw Thee die,
cried to Thee, O Christ, in bitter sorrow:
“Do not tarry among the dead, O Life!”

verses.psalm:
THEREFORE, I LOVE THY COMMANDMENTS
ABOVE GOLD, ABOVE FINE GOLD. (v. 127)
verses.troparion:
Hell, who had ﬁlled all men with fear,
trembled at the sight of Thee,
and in haste he yielded up his prisoners,
O Immortal Sun of Glory.

verses.psalm:
THEREFORE, I DIRECT MY STEPS BY ALL THY
PRECEPTS; I HATE EVERY FALSE WAY. (v. 128)
verses.troparion:
O Savior, the sight before our eyes
is great and terrible,
for the Cause of Life today submits to die,
wishing to give life to all.

verses.psalm:
THY TESTIMONIES ARE WONDERFUL;
THEREFORE MY SOUL SEEKS THEM. (v. 129)
verses.troparion:
O Master, Thy side is pierced
and Thy hands are nailed.
Thus, Thou hast healed the wounds of our
ﬁrst parents
and the sinful greed of their hands.

verses.psalm:
THE UNFOLDING OF THY WORDS GIVES
LIGHT; IT IMPARTS UNDERSTANDING TO
THE SIMPLE. (v. 130)
verses.troparion:
Once, in every house of Bethlehem,
they wept for Rachel’s children.
Now His Mother, with the apostolic choir,
laments for the Virgin’s Son.

verses.psalm:
WITH OPEN MOUTH I PANT, BECAUSE I
LONG FOR THY COMMANDMENTS. (v. 131)
verses.troparion:
Christ was slapped on the face
by the hands of mortal men,
though He fashioned man with His
almighty hand
and crushed the teeth of the Beast.

verses.psalm:
GLORY TO THE FATHER, AND TO THE SON,
AND TO THE HOLY SPIRIT.
verses.troparion:
All Thy faithful people, O Christ,
singing hymns of praise,
bless Thy cruciﬁxion and Thy burial,
by which we are ransomed from death.

verses.psalm:
NOW AND EVER, AND UNTO AGES OF AGES.
AMEN.
verses.troparion:
O God without beginning,
Eternal Word and Spirit,
help Thy people to prevail against the foe,
as Thou art the Lover of Mankind.

O Virgin, pure and undeﬁled,
you gave birth to our Life:
make the strife and scandals of the Church
to cease;
in your goodness, grant her peace.

stasis3.refrain: Every generation offers Thee its hymn of praise at Thy burial, O my Christ.

verses.psalm:
LOOK UPON ME, AND BE MERCIFUL TO ME,
AS IS THY GOOD PLEASURE TOWARD
THOSE WHO LOVE THY NAME. (v. 132)
verses.troparion:
Every generation
offers Thee its hymn of praise
at Thy burial, O my Christ.

verses.psalm:
ORDER MY STEPS ACCORDING TO THY
PROMISE, AND LET NO INIQUITY HAVE
DOMINION OVER ME. (v. 133)
verses.troparion:
The Arimathean
took Thee down from the tree
and laid Thee in a tomb.

verses.psalm:
DELIVER ME FROM THE FALSE ACCUSATION
OF MEN, THAT I MAY KEEP THY PRECEPTS.
(v. 134)
verses.troparion:
The myrrhbearing women
with foresight brought sweet spices
and drew near to Thee, O my Christ.

verses.psalm:
MAKE THY FACE TO SHINE UPON THY
SERVANT, AND TEACH ME THY STATUTES.
(v. 135)
verses.troparion:
Come, all creation,
sing a hymn to honor
the Creator’s burial.

verses.psalm:
MY EYES SHED STREAMS OF TEARS, BECAUSE
MEN DO NOT KEEP THY LAW. (v. 136)
verses.troparion:
Let us, with the myrrhbearers,
anoint as dead the Living One
with the Myrrh of True Knowledge.

verses.psalm:
RIGHTEOUS ART THOU, O LORD, AND
RIGHT ARE THY JUDGMENTS. (v. 137)
verses.troparion:
O thrice-blessed Joseph,
bury now the body
of Christ the Giver of Life.

verses.psalm:
THOU HAST APPOINTED THY TESTIMONIES
IN RIGHTEOUSNESS AND IN ALL
FAITHFULNESS. (v. 138)
verses.troparion:
Thou didst feed Thy people
with manna in the desert,
but they raised their heel against Thee.

verses.psalm:
MY ZEAL CONSUMES ME, BECAUSE MY FOES
FORGET THY WORDS. (v. 139)
verses.troparion:
Thou didst feed the nations,
ﬁlling them with bounty.
They repay Thee with gall and vinegar.

verses.psalm:
THY PROMISE IS WELL TRIED IN THE FIRE,
AND THY SERVANT LOVES IT. (v. 140)
verses.troparion:
Oh, the folly
of those who killed the prophets!
Now they slay the Messiah.

verses.psalm:
I AM SMALL AND DESPISED, YET I DO NOT
FORGET THY PRECEPTS. (v. 141)
verses.troparion:
Judas the disciple,
like a foolish servant,
has betrayed the Abyss of Wisdom.

verses.psalm:
THY RIGHTEOUSNESS IS RIGHTEOUSNESS
FOREVER, AND THY LAW IS TRUTH. (v. 142)
verses.troparion:
Judas the traitor
has become a captive
by selling his Deliverer.

verses.psalm:
TROUBLE AND ANGUISH HAVE COME
UPON ME, BUT THY COMMANDMENTS ARE
MY DELIGHT. (v. 143)
verses.troparion:
As Solomon has written,
the mouth of the transgressor
is a pit of evil.

verses.psalm:
THY TESTIMONIES ARE RIGHTEOUS
FOREVER; GIVE ME UNDERSTANDING THAT
I MAY LIVE. (v. 144)
verses.troparion:
God’s chosen people
chose to walk on crooked paths.
Thorns and snares are in their way!

verses.psalm:
I CRY WITH MY WHOLE HEART; HEAR ME, O
LORD! I WILL KEEP THY STATUTES. (v. 145)
verses.troparion:
Joseph and Nicodemus
bury the Creator
with honors ﬁtting for the dead.

verses.psalm:
I CRY TO THEE; SAVE ME, THAT I MAY
OBSERVE THY TESTIMONIES. (v. 146)
verses.troparion:
O Life-giving Savior,
Thou hast destroyed hell.
Unto Thee be might and glory!

verses.psalm:
I RISE BEFORE DAWN AND CRY FOR HELP; I
HOPE IN THY WORDS. (v. 147)
verses.troparion:
The All-pure Virgin wept
with a mother’s grief, O Word,
when she saw Thee lying dead.

verses.psalm:
MY EYES ARE AWAKE BEFORE THE
MORNING, THAT I MAY MEDITATE UPON
THY PROMISE. (v. 148)
verses.troparion:
“O my sweet springtime,
O my sweetest Child,
where has all Thy beauty faded?”

verses.psalm:
HEAR MY VOICE ACCORDING TO THY
LOVINGKINDNESS, O LORD; IN THY
JUDGMENT GIVE ME LIFE. (v. 149)
verses.troparion:
Thine All-pure Mother
mourned and lamented Thee
when she saw Thee dead, O Word.

verses.psalm:
THEY DRAW NEAR WHO PERSECUTE ME
WITH EVIL PURPOSE; THEY ARE FAR FROM
THY LAW. (v. 150)
verses.troparion:
The myrrhbearing women
came at dawn with spices
to anoint Christ, the Myrrh of God.

verses.psalm:
BUT THOU ART NEAR, O LORD, AND ALL
THY COMMANDMENTS ARE TRUE. (v. 151)
verses.troparion:
By dying, O my God,
Thou hast put death to death,
through Thy divine power.

verses.psalm:
LONG HAVE I KNOWN FROM THY
TESTIMONIES, THAT THOU HAST FOUNDED
THEM FOREVER. (v. 152)
verses.troparion:
The Deceiver has been deceived,
and those whom he deceived are freed
by Thy Wisdom, O God.

verses.psalm:
LOOK ON MY HUMILIATION AND DELIVER
ME, FOR I DO NOT FORGET THY LAW.
(v. 153)
verses.troparion:
The traitor has thrown himself
down to the depths of hell
and the pit of destruction.

verses.psalm:
JUDGE MY CAUSE, AND REDEEM ME; GIVE
ME LIFE ACCORDING TO THY PROMISE.
(v. 154)
verses.troparion:
Foolish and most wretched,
Judas chose an evil path.
Thorns and snares are in his way!

verses.psalm:
SALVATION IS FAR FROM THE WICKED, FOR
THEY DO NOT SEEK THY STATUTES. (v. 155)
verses.troparion:
All who cruciﬁed Thee, O Word,
shall be destroyed together
in the pit of destruction.

All bloodguilty men
shall be destroyed together
in the pit of destruction.

verses.psalm:
MANY ARE MY PERSECUTORS AND MY
ADVERSARIES, BUT I DO NOT SWERVE
FROM THY TESTIMONIES. (v. 157)
verses.troparion:
O Son of God and King of all,
my God and my Creator,
how canst Thou endure such suffering?

verses.psalm:
I LOOK AT THE FAITHLESS WITH DISGUST,
BECAUSE THEY DO NOT KEEP THY
COMMANDMENTS. (v. 158)
verses.troparion:
As a ewe beholds her lamb,
the Virgin gazed on Thee with grief
when she saw Thee hanging on the tree.

verses.psalm:
CONSIDER HOW I LOVE THY PRECEPTS!
GIVE ME LIFE ACCORDING TO THY MERCY.
(v. 159)
verses.troparion:
Joseph and Nicodemus
bury the Body
that gives life to all the world.

verses.psalm:
THE SUM OF THY WORD IS TRUTH, AND
EVERY ONE OF THY RIGHTEOUS
ORDINANCES ENDURES FOREVER. (v. 160)
verses.troparion:
The Virgin’s heart was pierced.
She shed hot tears for Thee,
and cried out lamenting.

verses.psalm:
PRINCES PERSECUTE ME WITHOUT CAUSE,
BUT MY HEART STANDS IN AWE OF THY
WORDS. (v. 161)
verses.troparion:
“O Light of my eyes,
O my beloved Son,
how art Thou now hidden in a tomb?”

verses.psalm:
I REJOICE AT THY WORD LIKE ONE WHO
FINDS GREAT SPOIL. (v. 162)
verses.troparion:
“Do not weep, O Mother!
I suffered all these things
to give freedom to Adam and Eve.”

verses.psalm:
I HATE AND ABHOR FALSEHOOD, BUT I
LOVE THY LAW. (V. 163)
verses.troparion:
“O my Son, I praise Thee
for Thy great compassion
that led Thee to this suffering!”

verses.psalm:
SEVEN TIMES A DAY I PRAISE THEE FOR THY
RIGHTEOUS ORDINANCES. (v. 164)
verses.troparion:
Thou hast tasted vinegar
to free us from the bitter taste
of the fruit forbidden of old.

verses.psalm:
GREAT PEACE HAVE THOSE WHO LOVE THY
LAW; NOTHING CAN MAKE THEM STUMBLE.
(v. 165)
verses.troparion:
Thou art nailed upon the cross,
who of old didst shelter
Thy people with a pillar of cloud.

verses.psalm:
I HOPE FOR THY SALVATION, O LORD, AND
I LOVE THY COMMANDMENTS. (v. 166)
verses.troparion:
The myrrhbearing women
came, O Savior, to Thy tomb,
offering Thee sweet spices.

verses.psalm:
MY SOUL KEEPS THY TESTIMONIES; I LOVE
THEM EXCEEDINGLY. (v. 167)
verses.troparion:
Arise, O Lord all-merciful!
Raise us from the depths of hell
and from the dark abyss of sin.

verses.psalm:
I KEEP THY PRECEPTS AND TESTIMONIES,
FOR ALL MY WAYS ARE BEFORE THEE, O
LORD. (v. 168)
verses.troparion:
The Mother who gave birth to Thee
prayed to Thee with weeping:
“Arise, O Lord, and Giver of Life!”

Make haste to arise, O Word!
Take away the sorrow
of Thy Virgin Mother.

All the hosts of heaven
were ﬁlled with fear and wonder
when they saw Thee dead, O Lord.

verses.psalm:
MY LIPS WILL POUR FORTH PRAISE, FOR
THOU HAST TAUGHT ME THY STATUTES.
(v. 171)
verses.troparion:
We honor Thy passion
with love and fear, O Lord.
Grant us forgiveness of our sins.

verses.psalm:
MY TONGUE WILL SING OF THY WORD,
FOR ALL THY COMMANDMENTS ARE RIGHT.
(v. 172)
verses.troparion:
O strange and dreadful wonder!
How art Thou now hidden
in the earth, O Word of God?

verses.psalm:
LET THY HAND BE NEAR TO SAVE ME, FOR I
HAVE CHOSEN THY PRECEPTS. (v. 173)
verses.troparion:
When Thou didst ﬂee to Egypt,
Joseph guarded Thee, O Lord.
Now another Joseph buries Thee.

verses.psalm:
I LONG FOR THY SALVATION, O LORD, AND
THY LAW IS MY DELIGHT. (v. 174)
verses.troparion:
Thine All-holy Mother
weeps for Thee, lamenting
at Thy death, O my Savior.

verses.psalm:
LET MY SOUL LIVE, THAT I MAY PRAISE THEE,
AND LET THINE ORDINANCES HELP ME.
(v. 175)
verses.troparion:
The hosts of angels tremble
at the strange and fearful sight
of Thy burial, O Maker of all.

verses.psalm:
I HAVE GONE ASTRAY LIKE A LOST SHEEP;
SEEK THY SERVANT, FOR I HAVE NOT
FORGOTTEN THY COMMANDMENTS.
(v. 176)
verses.troparion:
Early in the morning
the myrrhbearing women
came to Thee and sprinkled myrrh on Thy
tomb.

verses.psalm:
GLORY TO THE FATHER, AND TO THE SON,
AND TO THE HOLY SPIRIT.
verses.troparion:
By Thy resurrection,
grant peace to Thy Church,
and salvation to Thy people.

verses.psalm:
NOW AND EVER, AND UNTO AGES OF AGES.
AMEN.
verses.troparion:
O God in Trinity,
Father, Son, and Spirit,
grant Thy mercy to the world.

Grant us, your servants,
to behold, O Virgin,
the Resurrection of your Son!

smallLitanies._note: Small litanies (ektenias) after each stasis. Each has a different priestly exclamation.

petitions.opening: Again and again, in peace, let us pray to the Lord.
petitions.helpUs: Help us, save us, have mercy on us, and keep us, O God, by Thy grace.
petitions.commemoration: Calling to remembrance our most holy, most pure, most blessed, glorious Lady Theotokos and Ever-Virgin Mary with all the saints, let us commit ourselves and one another and all our life unto Christ our God.

afterStasis1.exclamation: For blessed is Thy Name and glorified is Thy Kingdom; of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit, now and ever, and unto the ages of ages.

afterStasis2.exclamation: For holy art Thou, our God, who restest upon the glorious throne of the cherubim, and unto Thee do we send up glory, together with Thine unoriginate Father and Thy most holy, good and life-creating Spirit, now and ever, and unto the ages of ages.

afterStasis3._note: Some traditions omit the small litany after Stasis 3 and proceed directly to the Evlogetaria. OCA practice typically includes it.
afterStasis3.exclamation: For Thou art the King of peace, O Christ our God, and unto Thee we send up glory, together with Thine unoriginate Father, and Thy most holy, good and life-creating Spirit, now and ever, and unto the ages of ages.

evlogetaria._note: Resurrectional Evlogitaria. Tone 5. Sung after the three stases.
evlogetaria.refrain: Blessed art Thou, O Lord, teach me Thy statutes!

troparia.verse: Blessed art Thou, O Lord, teach me Thy statutes!
troparia.text:
The angelic host was filled with awe
when it saw Thee among the dead.
By destroying the power of death, O Savior,
Thou didst raise Adam
and save all men from Hell.

troparia.verse: Blessed art Thou, O Lord, teach me Thy statutes!
troparia.text:
In the tomb the radiant angel cried to the myrrh-bearers:
"Why do you women mingle myrrh with your tears?
Look at the tomb and understand!
The Savior is risen from the dead."

troparia.verse: Blessed art Thou, O Lord, teach me Thy statutes!
troparia.text:
Very early in the morning the myrrh-bearers ran with sorrow to Thy tomb.
But an angel came to them and said:
"The time for sorrow has come to an end.
Do not weep, but announce the resurrection to the Apostles."

troparia.verse: Blessed art Thou, O Lord, teach me Thy statutes!
troparia.text:
The myrrh-bearers were sorrowful as they neared Thy tomb,
but the angel said to them:
"Why do you number the living among the dead?
Since He is God, He is risen from the tomb."

troparia.verse: Glory to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit.
troparia.text:
We worship the Father, and His Son, and the Holy Spirit,
the Holy Trinity, one in essence.
We cry with the Seraphim:
"Holy, Holy, Holy art Thou, O Lord."

troparia.verse: Now and ever, and unto ages of ages. Amen.
troparia.text:
Since you gave birth to the Giver of Life, O Virgin,
you delivered Adam from his sin.
You gave joy to Eve instead of sadness.
The God-man who was born of you
has restored to life those who had fallen from it.

evlogetaria.alleluia: Alleluia, Alleluia, Alleluia, glory to Thee, O God. (×3)

canon._note: The Great Canon of Holy Saturday. Abbreviated — full odes would be very lengthy.
canon.rubric: The Canon of Holy Saturday is chanted here. The first, third, fourth, and ODES of this canon are the work of Mark the Monk, Bishop of Hydrous, and the last four ODES are by Kosmas of the Holy City. The Irmoi are the work of the nun Kassiani.

kontakion.text:
He who shut in the depths is beheld dead,
wrapped in fine linen and spices.
The Immortal One is laid in a tomb as a mortal man.
The women have come to anoint Him with myrrh,
weeping bitterly and crying:
"This is the most blessed sabbath,
on which Christ has fallen asleep to rise on the third day."

ikos.text:
He who holds all things together has been lifted up upon the cross,
and all of creation weeps at seeing Him hanging naked upon the wood.
The sun hid its rays and the stars cast aside their splendor.
The earth shook with great fear, the sea fled,
and the rocks were split asunder.
Many tombs were opened, and the bodies of holy men arose.
Hell groaned below, and the Jews considered how to slander the resurrection of Christ,
but the women cried:
"This is the most blessed sabbath,
on which Christ has fallen asleep to rise on the third day."

exaposteilarion.label: Exaposteilarion
exaposteilarion.text: Holy is the Lord our God!
exaposteilarion._note: Sung three times, with the verse 'For He is holy' and 'Exalt the Lord our God.'

lauds._note: Lauds stichera. Tone 2. Sung with 'Let every breath praise the Lord.'

hymns.verse: Let every breath praise the Lord. Praise the Lord from the heavens; praise Him in the highest!
hymns.text:
Every creature was altered by Thy Passion, O Lord;
all things suffered with Thee, knowing that Thou holdest all things together.

hymns.verse: Praise Him for His mighty acts; praise Him according to the multitude of His greatness!
hymns.text:
The Giver of the Law lies dead; a lawless people has condemned the Lawgiver.
O new wonders! The Judge is judged, yet He wills to suffer and to die for the salvation of the world.

hymns.verse: Praise Him with the sound of the trumpet; praise Him with the psaltery and harp!
hymns.text:
O Lord, when the myrrh-bearing women stood at Thy tomb,
and the soldiers guarded Thy most pure Body,
Thou didst plunder hell and condemn the demons,
rising as Victor on the third day.

hymns.verse: Praise Him with timbrel and dance; praise Him with strings and instruments!
hymns.text:
O Lord, the hell that had swallowed the Cornerstone
has released its dead;
and the tomb has given up for judgment those whom it held of old.
The Giver of Life has shut up death, and the Lord has poured His light on all.

glory.text:
Today a tomb holds Him Who holds all creation in the hollow of His hand.
A stone covers Him Who covers the heavens with virtue.
Life sleeps, and hell trembles, and Adam is set free from his bonds.
Glory to Thy dispensation, whereby, having accomplished all things,
Thou hast granted us an eternal Sabbath,
Thy most holy Resurrection from the dead!

now.text:
Most blessed art thou, O Virgin Theotokos,
for through Him Who was incarnate of thee,
Hades is led captive,
Adam recalled, the curse destroyed,
Eve freed, death slain,
and we are given life.
Wherefore, we cry out in praise:
Blessed art Thou, O Christ our God, Who art thus well pleased! Glory to Thee!

greatDoxology._note: The Great Doxology is SUNG (not read) at the Lamentations service.
greatDoxology.text:
Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace, good will among men.

We praise Thee, we bless Thee, we worship Thee, we glorify Thee, we give thanks to Thee for Thy great glory.

O Lord God, heavenly King, God the Father Almighty; O Lord, the only-begotten Son, Jesus Christ; and O Holy Spirit.

O Lord God, Lamb of God, Son of the Father, that takest away the sin of the world, have mercy on us; Thou that takest away the sins of the world, receive our prayer; Thou that sittest at the right hand of the Father, have mercy on us.

For Thou only art holy, Thou only art the Lord, Jesus Christ, to the glory of God the Father. Amen.

Every day will I bless Thee, and I will praise Thy Name forever and ever.

Vouchsafe, O Lord, to keep us this day without sin.

Blessed art Thou, O Lord, the God of our fathers, and praised and glorified is Thy Name forever. Amen.

Let Thy mercy, O Lord, be upon us, as we have set our hope on Thee.

Blessed art Thou, O Lord; teach me Thy statutes.
Blessed art Thou, O Master; make me to understand Thy statutes.
Blessed art Thou, O Holy One; enlighten me with Thy statutes.

Thy mercy, O Lord, endureth forever. O despise not the works of Thy hands.

To Thee belongeth worship, to Thee belongeth praise, to Thee belongeth glory: to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit, now and ever and unto ages of ages. Amen.

trisagion._note: Sung during the procession with the Epitaphios, after the Great Doxology.
trisagion.text:
Holy God, Holy Mighty, Holy Immortal, have mercy on us. (×3)

Glory to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit, now and ever and unto ages of ages. Amen.

Holy Immortal, have mercy on us.

Holy God, Holy Mighty, Holy Immortal, have mercy on us.

procession._note: The Epitaphios procession takes place after the Great Doxology. The Trisagion is sung during the procession.
procession.rubric: The Great Doxology is sung. At its conclusion the priest takes up the Epitaphios (burial shroud of Christ) and carries it in procession around the church, while the Trisagion is chanted.

prophecy._note: After the procession, the prophecy from Ezekiel is read.
prophecy.label: Prophecy of Ezekiel
prophecy.text: The hand of the Lord was upon me, and carried me out in the Spirit of the Lord, and set me down in the midst of the valley which was full of bones, and caused me to pass by them round about: and, behold, there were very many in the open valley; and, lo, they were very dry. And He said unto me: Son of man, can these bones live? And I answered: O Lord God, Thou knowest. Again He said unto me: Prophesy upon these bones, and say unto them: O ye dry bones, hear the word of the Lord. Thus saith the Lord God unto these bones: Behold, I will cause breath to enter into you, and ye shall live: and I will lay sinews upon you, and will bring up flesh upon you, and cover you with skin, and put breath in you, and ye shall live; and ye shall know that I am the Lord. So I prophesied as I was commanded: and as I prophesied, there was a noise, and behold a shaking, and the bones came together, bone to his bone. And when I beheld, lo, the sinews and the flesh came up upon them, and the skin covered them above: but there was no breath in them. Then said He unto me: Prophesy unto the wind, prophesy, son of man, and say to the wind: Thus saith the Lord God: Come from the four winds, O breath, and breathe upon these slain, that they may live. So I prophesied as He commanded me, and the breath came into them, and they lived, and stood up upon their feet, an exceeding great army. Then He said unto me: Son of man, these bones are the whole house of Israel: behold, they say: Our bones are dried, and our hope is lost; we are cut off for our parts. Therefore prophesy and say unto them: Thus saith the Lord God: Behold, O My people, I will open your graves, and cause you to come up out of your graves, and bring you into the land of Israel. And ye shall know that I am the Lord, when I have opened your graves, O My people, and brought you up out of your graves, and shall put My Spirit in you, and ye shall live, and I shall place you in your own land: then shall ye know that I the Lord have spoken it, and performed it, saith the Lord.

prokeimenon.refrain: Arise, O Lord, help us, and redeem us for Thy mercy's sake.
prokeimenon.verse: O God, we have heard with our ears, and our fathers have told us.

epistle.text:
Brethren: Know ye not that a little leaven leaveneth the whole lump? Purge out therefore the old leaven, that ye may be a new lump, as ye are unleavened. For even Christ our Passover is sacrificed for us. Therefore let us keep the feast, not with old leaven, neither with the leaven of malice and wickedness, but with the unleavened bread of sincerity and truth.

Christ hath redeemed us from the curse of the law, being made a curse for us: for it is written, Cursed is every one that hangeth on a tree: that the blessing of Abraham might come on the Gentiles through Jesus Christ; that we might receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.

verses: Arise, O Lord my God; let Thy hand be lifted high; forget not Thy poor forever.
I will give thanks unto Thee, O Lord, with my whole heart; I will tell of all Thy wondrous works.

gospel.label: The Sealing of the Tomb
gospel.text: Now the next day, that followed the day of the preparation, the chief priests and Pharisees came together unto Pilate, saying: Sir, we remember that that deceiver said, while He was yet alive: After three days I will rise again. Command therefore that the sepulchre be made sure until the third day, lest His disciples come by night, and steal Him away, and say unto the people: He is risen from the dead; so the last error shall be worse than the first. Pilate said unto them: Ye have a watch; go your way, make it as sure as ye can. So they went, and made the sepulchre sure, sealing the stone, and setting a watch.

dismissal.text: May He Who for our salvation endured the spitting, and the scourging, and the buffeting, and the Cross, and death, and was laid in the tomb, Christ our true God, through the prayers of His all-pure Mother, and of all the saints, have mercy on us and save us, for He is good and loveth mankind.
